mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) signals a tightening monetary policy, which raises the cost of capital and discount rates for future earnings, leading to a negative impact on equity valuations and S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 3.78% in Nov 2022, indicating ongoing tightening
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns show no significant negative correlation with FEDFUNDS changes during periods when FEDFUNDS has increased by more than 100 basis points over 6 months.

mechanism_narrative: Persistently high consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) above a critical threshold indicates ongoing inflationary pressures, forcing the Federal Reserve to maintain or intensify restrictive monetary policies, which negatively impacts corporate earnings and equity valuations over a longer horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 298.349 in Oct 2022, indicating high inflation
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive or flat in more than 40% of instances where CPIAUCSL remains above 290 for 3 consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of low unemployment (UNRATE) and consistent job creation (PAYEMS) signifies a robust labor market, which supports strong consumer spending and corporate profits, thereby driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 3.7% and PAYEMS at 153.5M in Nov 2022, indicating strong labor market
failure_condition: When UNRATE is below 4% and PAYEMS growth is positive for 3 consecutive months, SPX 20-day returns are negative more than 50% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: A significant month-over-month decline in retail sales (RSAFS) signals weakening consumer demand and confidence, directly impacting corporate revenues and earnings expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns in the very short term.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are positive or flat in more than 40% of instances where RSAFS declines by more than 1.0% month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in industrial production (INDPRO) indicates a healthy and expanding manufacturing sector, which typically translates to higher corporate earnings and overall economic strength, driving positive equity market performance over a longer horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns show no significant positive correlation with INDPRO growth over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in housing starts (HOUST) combined with a rising federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) signals a severe slowdown in the housing market and broader economic activity, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns as investor sentiment deteriorates.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines > 10% month-over-month AND FEDFUNDS has risen > 0.5% in 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1425.0 in Oct 2022, FEDFUNDS at 3.78% in Nov 2022
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are positive or flat in more than 35% of instances where HOUST declines significantly during periods of rising FEDFUNDS.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) rises aggressively while inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains elevated, it indicates an intense monetary tightening cycle aimed at curbing inflation, which typically leads to a contraction in economic activity and negative equity market performance.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises > 0.75% in 3 months AND CPIAUCSL > 295
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 3.78% and CPIAUCSL at 298.349 in Nov 2022
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive or flat in more than 30% of instances where FEDFUNDS has risen by more than 0.75% over 3 months while CPIAUCSL remains above 295.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) after a prolonged period of low unemployment often signals a potential bottoming of the economic cycle and anticipation of future monetary easing, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.4pp in 3 months from its 12-month low
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns remain negative or flat for more than 6 months after UNRATE rises by 0.4 percentage points from its 12-month low.
